mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below -0.02 or positive average 20-day returns in events triggered by UNRATE > 5.0% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenues and equity prices, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Information coefficient (IC) below 0.05 over a rolling 2-year period for the 5-day forward return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are above their 12-month moving averages, the economy is operating at high capacity, boosting corporate profits and supporting a positive 60-day equity outlook.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both above their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Negative average 60-day returns when both triggers are active, or less than 60% of positive returns in such windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) exceeds 11,500 billion chained dollars, the economy is in a mature expansion where further growth decelerates, leading to lower equity returns over the next 20 trading days due to diminishing cyclical momentum.
failure_condition: Positive average 20-day returns in all quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 11500, or Rank IC above -0.01 in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,500 thousand units signals a weakening housing sector that depresses residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Median 60-day return positive in events triggered by HOUST < 1500, or Sharpe ratio below -0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Information coefficient below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or negative average 5-day returns in any consecutive 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity that presages lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive median 20-day return in all episodes where INDPRO < 110, or hit rate below 55% of negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 4.5% and the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, the economy faces late-cycle overheating that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative 60-day returns as monetary tightening weighs on growth.
failure_condition: Positive average 60-day returns in triggered windows, or less than 40% of such windows produce negative returns over a 10-year backtest.
